calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Remaining Issues From The First And Final Report And Account Regarding Statutory Fees
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Clifford Fried for Objector
Clinton Killian for Petitioner

The Court noted statutory fees are the remaining issue today, and that $1,775.35 is unaccounted for by Mr. Fried's firm. Mr. Fried concedes that $1,773.35 can be returned, notes that his exhbiits E,I, and J show reconciliation of accounts and presents an email attachment to exhibit I which was inadvertently left out of the filed document. Mr. Killian stated that the agreement was to split the fees with the firm 50-50 from 6/2016-4/2017, that he was independent from 4/2017 to the present, and request Ms. Manzanillo's payments for administrative statutory fees to be repaid and distributed to the two heirs. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to payment of statutory fees, reconciliation of accounting, credit/lack of credit to Ms. Manzanillo's payments, invoices, and billing for secretarial services. The matter was taken under submission. The Court will allow Mr. Fried to submit a proposed order to be considered with Mr. Killian's proposed order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Remaining Issues From The First And Final Report And Account Regarding Statutory Fees
Off Calendar Nov-07-2018 Continued To Nov-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Clinton Killian for Petitioner.
Clifford Fried and Jonathan Madison on behalf of Fried & Williams (Objector).

The Court stated that it appears all parties got paid their fees without prior court order. Mr. Fried stated that this is incorrect, that he is not sure the fees collected were for statutory fees, and that there were other issues they addressed such as the quiet title, a spousal petition, petition to determine heirship, etc. Mr. Fried also stated that he believes the funds received were applied to extraordinary fees, that there was a reconciliation and that there was an agreement to split statutory fees 50/50. Mr. Messina stated that he spoke with Mr. Killian, that Mr. Killian explained why the extraordinary fees were that amount, that he presented it to his Client and that his Client wants this resolved quicker rather than later. Mr. Killian stated that the Fried & Williams firm billed his Client directly and that his Client paid over $10,000.00 to the Fried firm. No one objects to the Court's suggestion of bifurcating the matter and deciding all issues other than the statutory fees. The First and Final Report and Account, etc. is granted in part. The remaining issues regarding statutory fees (amount to be withheld from distribution) is continued to November 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Fried is to respond to Ms. Manzanillo's declaration by October 19, 2018. Mr. Killian is to reply by October 26, 2018. If Mr. Messina wants to address the issue of the Court having authority to reduce statutory fees, he is to file his pleading by October 19, 2018. Mr. Killian is to provide a revised order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Clinton Killian for Petitioner.
Richard Shearer for Carole Molloy (Respondent).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the parties are required to comply with Probate law when selling the house, or whether they are to follow instructions provided in the civil partition action. The Petition for Instructions, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Abate Administrator Maria Raquel Manzanillos Petition To Obtain Possession Of Personal Property Under Probate Code Section 850
Off Calendar Apr-05-2017 Text Ruling

Petition To Obtain Possession Of Personal Property Under Probate Code Sec. 850
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Clinton Killian for Maria Manzanillo (Administrator/Petitioner).
Richard Shearer for Carole Molloy (Objector).

Mr. Shearer stated that he has been involved in this case for hours, that he is not in a position to know what to do with this case, that the matter should be abated or he needs a significant continuance (six months), and that the other matter (civil) is being litigated. Mr. Killian stated that this is now the fourth attorney from which he has requested an accounting, that he received no response from July through October, that rents are being collected and that there was one form interrogatory that was not answered. Mr. Shearer also stated that he forwarded all information from Ms. Steyer's office, suggested Mr. Killian call Ms. Steyer, stated that no new issues are being presented in this 850 Petition and that the same issues can't be requested in both matters. The Court ordered an accounting from date of death to present. The parties are to provide information. The accounting is to be provided to the Administrator by March 21, 2017. All other issues will be preserved for a later decision. The balance of the petition is continued to April 11, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Killian will file a status report. The Petition to Obtain Possession of Personal Property is granted only as to the request for an accounting (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Heirship

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Clinton Killian for Maria Manzanillo. Dennis Messina for (heir) Daniel Urquico.

The Court stated that this is a surviving spouse petition for letters of administration, that Daniel has issues regarding heirship and confirmed that he is not objecting to the issuance of letters. Mr. Killian stated that there is no one else involved and that counsel has agreed that Mr. Messina will file a petition for heirship. The Court stated that it will grant the petition, and that it needs to be with either full bond or limited authority. Mr. Killian stated that his client is not in the possession of real property, that it was originally owned by 4 people, that title is clouded, that they are not collecting rent and that the estate has no assets. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration with limited authority and no bond (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
